title: ServiceNow, Inc. (NOW) Intrinsic Value
description: An independent discounted cash flow (DCF) intrinsic value analysis of ServiceNow, Inc. (NOW) compiled entirely by Gemini 3.1.

fcfGrowth.title: FCF Growth Rate (Y1-Y5)
fcfGrowth.rationale: A 24% CAGR over five years is exceptionally aggressive for a company of this scale, but ServiceNow’s land-and-expand model, high gross margins, and massive total addressable market (TAM) expansion into customer and creator workflows make this entirely achievable as operating leverage continues to kick in.

discountRate.title: Discount Rate (WACC)
discountRate.rationale: <div class="assumption-grid" data-astro-cid-xyujfwu6> <div class="assumption-card" data-astro-cid-xyujfwu6> <div class="card-title" data-astro-cid-xyujfwu6>FCF Growth Rate (Y1-Y5)

terminalGrowth.title: Terminal Growth Rate
terminalGrowth.rationale: 4.0% reflects ServiceNow's ability to durably outpace broad economic growth. Software workflows are deflationary to corporate expenses, guaranteeing an ongoing, structural shift of enterprise budgets toward ServiceNow's platform long after hyper-growth ends.

thesisTitle: The AI Thesis: The Operating System of the Enterprise
thesisParagraphs: ServiceNow has transcended IT service management (ITSM) to become the central workflow engine for the world's largest enterprises. It commands exceptional pricing power and world-class net revenue retention (NRR) rates. However, with massive growth already priced into the stock, a meticulous DCF is crucial to determine if the fundamental cash flow generation justifies the current enterprise value.

With the integration of generative AI (Now Assist) directly into their Pro Plus tiers, ServiceNow isn't just selling software—it's selling labor efficiency. This presents a unique inflection point for margin expansion and sustained hyper-growth in Free Cash Flow over the coming decade.
verdictFooter: Based on FY2025 actuals. Data via S&P Global (Mar 18, 2026).
